claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you some questions. Remember, Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each child individually.

**First question: Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children that I'm inquiring about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll assess eligibility for each of your three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie - individually.

Let me start with Alex.

**Regarding Alex: How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Alex is 3 years old, which means they're under 16.

**Regarding Alex: Is Alex working full-time (meaning 24 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're only 3!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Of course, that makes sense! Now let me ask about your second child.

**Regarding Blake: How old is Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Blake is 8 years old, which means they're also under 16.

**Regarding Blake: Is Blake working full-time (meaning 24 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't work full-time. They're just 8, so they're still in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now let me ask about your third child.

**Regarding Charlie: How old is Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie is 2 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Charlie is 2 years old, so also under 16.

**Regarding Charlie: Is Charlie working full-time (meaning 24 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Charlie doesn't work - they're only 2!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you some questions about your own circumstances and your relationship with the children.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Are any of your three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) receiving benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them receive any benefits in their own right.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) in an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in an apprenticeship. They're all too young for that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. You've confirmed you live in the UK. Now I need to understand your relationship with the children.

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie? This typically means they live with you, or you're contributing at least the Child Benefit rate towards their upkeep if they don't live with you.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for all three of them. They all live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently:**
- In hospital for treatment?
- In local authority care?
- Being fostered?

Or are they all living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me. None of them are in hospital, care, or being fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards any of your children's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's a summary of why each child qualifies:

**Alex (age 3):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Not working full-time ✓
- Not receiving benefits in their own right ✓
- Not in an apprenticeship ✓
- Lives with you and you're responsible for them ✓
- Living normally at home ✓

**Blake (age 8):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Not working full-time ✓
- Not receiving benefits in their own right ✓
- Not in an apprenticeship ✓
- Lives with you and you're responsible for them ✓
- Living normally at home ✓

**Charlie (age 2):**
- Under 16 years old ✓
- Not working full-time ✓
- Not receiving benefits in their own right ✓
- Not in an apprenticeship ✓
- Lives with you and you're responsible for them ✓
- Living normally at home ✓

**Next steps to claim:**
- Apply online or by post using form CH2
- Provide proof of identity for yourself and each child
- Provide each child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
- Be aware that if you or your partner earns over £60,000, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply
- Once approved, payments are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much! You've been really helpful. I now know I'm eligible and what I need to do to claim. I appreciate your time!
